note: A Dutch study testing a school-based support program for neurodiverse teens who struggle to attend. One of the more practically grounded intervention papers I've come across — useful for thinking about what 'support' actually looks like in a school setting.

An Australian online parenting program for families of adolescents who refuse school. Interesting because parents found it helpful and acceptable — but school attendance didn't actually improve. Raises real questions about whether parent-only approaches are sufficient.
A UK feasibility study of a coach-supported online program for school anxiety and absenteeism. Early-stage work, but a good example of how digital delivery is being tested in this space.

Looks at personality traits in adolescents with school refusal and whether they predict how well treatment works. A reminder that 'school refusal' is not one thing — who the young person is matters enormously for outcomes.

Teachers' experiences of a Norwegian school absenteeism intervention. What struck me here was how much coordination burden falls on school staff — a factor that rarely makes it into intervention design.
A recent systematic review of intervention programs for school attendance problems more broadly. Good for situating the EBSA-specific literature within the wider attendance field.

A foundational practitioner review of school refusal — covers how thinking in this area has shifted since 2000. Still one of the clearest accounts of why definitions matter so much for how we design and evaluate interventions.

From colleagues at RMIT — uses network analysis to map the relationships between sexual addiction and gambling disorder. A different area from my own work, but a nice example of how complex behavioural patterns can be modelled without assuming simple cause and effect.
Empirical work on AI systems and creative performance. Focuses on computational approaches to modelling creativity rather than clinical or educational application.
Conceptual synthesis on AI and creativity. Emphasises theoretical mechanisms and broader implications rather than applied intervention work.
Applied cognitive research on AI-assisted creativity. Relevant for understanding decision processes under human–machine collaboration.
Autism-focused early intervention study examining downstream effects on school anxiety and attendance-related outcomes. Preventive rather than adolescent EBSA treatment work.
Early clinical discussion of school refusal and truancy. Historically important but conceptually dated relative to modern EBSA frameworks.
Proof-of-concept virtual reality exposure intervention for school anxiety. Experimental stage with limited generalisability.
Structured re-engagement program (“The Link”) for school-refusing adolescents. Focus on reintegration rather than symptom reduction alone.
SWiM guideline for narrative synthesis in systematic reviews. Methodological standard for non-meta-analytic evidence synthesis.
Review of modifiable parental variables associated with school refusal. Highlights family-level intervention targets.
Early adaptation of DBT principles for school refusal with digital coaching elements. Prototype-stage intervention development.
Examines autism-related traits associated with school refusal patterns. Supports heterogeneity in mechanisms underpinning attendance problems.
Narrative synthesis of clinical presentations linked to school refusal. Emphasises comorbidity and heterogeneity over single-cause models.
Home–school–clinic re-engagement model integrating education and mental health services. Multi-system coordination emphasis.
Student accounts of EBSA in specialist settings. Focus on perceived helpful conditions for re-engagement.
Conceptual clarification of school refusal definitions. Focus on boundaries between related attendance constructs.
Framing article introducing classification and intervention distinctions in attendance problems literature.
Clarifies distinctions between types of school attendance problems. Foundational for current EBSA classification debates.
Reviews constructs used in post-intervention EBSA research. Identifies inconsistency in outcome measurement frameworks.
Conceptual clarification of school refusal definitions. Focuses on distinguishing constructs within school attendance problems and implications for intervention design.
Introductory framing paper for a special series on school attendance problems. Emphasises classification issues and how they shape intervention logic.
Key paper differentiating categories of school attendance problems. Central to modern EBSA taxonomy debates.
Examines constructs used to measure outcomes after EBSA interventions. Identifies lack of consistency in outcome selection and reporting.
Practitioner-focused review of adolescent school refusal with emphasis on CBT manuals and developmental considerations.
School-based intervention for neurodiverse adolescents with attendance difficulties. Highlights multi-stakeholder coordination across school, family, and student systems.
Cluster RCT of mindfulness-based yoga for school refusal. Reports physiological and anxiety-related outcomes rather than attendance change alone.
Standardised assessment scale for school refusal patterns (SRAS-R). Widely used in classification and functional assessment approaches.
Foundational clinical review of school refusal and absenteeism. Establishes early integrative models still referenced in current frameworks.
Proposes integrated systemic and analytic model for understanding school attendance problems. Moves away from single-cause explanations.
Brief review of chronic absenteeism causes and treatment approaches. Broader than EBSA but commonly used in related synthesis.
Early review of school refusal in child and adolescent psychiatry. Establishes clinical baseline for later intervention literature.
Feasibility study of coach-assisted online intervention for school anxiety and absenteeism. Early-stage evidence on digital delivery models.
Online parenting intervention for emotionally based school avoidance. High acceptability but limited behavioural impact on attendance outcomes.
Rapid return school-based CBT intervention in adolescent sample. Focus on structured reintegration into school attendance.
Systematic review and meta-analysis of interventions for school refusal. Suggests overall positive effects but highlights weak methodological consistency across studies.
Co-designed parent-focused intervention for emotionally based school avoidance. Emphasises acceptability and feasibility over efficacy at this stage.
Multi-disciplinary home–school–clinic model for school-refusing youth. Focuses on coordinated service delivery across education and clinical systems.
RCT combining CBT with fluoxetine for school refusal. Suggests potential additive benefit but raises questions about pharmacological contribution.
KiTeS bioecological framework for understanding school attendance problems. Positions EBSA within multi-layered environmental systems.
Examines ASD-related characteristics associated with school refusal. Reinforces heterogeneity in underlying mechanisms.
Protocol for post-psychiatric inpatient reintegration program. Focus on structured return-to-school pathways after severe mental health episodes.
Links bullying experiences with school refusal in autistic children. Suggests social environment as a key mediating factor.
Explores supportive factors for autistic girls re-engaging in mainstream schooling. Emphasises contextual and relational supports.
Clinician perspectives on how education systems can support child mental health. Focuses on system-level rather than individual interventions.
Broad review of interventions for school attendance problems. Useful for situating EBSA-specific interventions within wider attendance literature.
Randomised controlled trial of treatment approaches for school avoidance in clinical populations. Early evidence base with limited generalisability.
Qualitative evidence on multifamily therapy for school refusal. Highlights perceived benefits in family communication and support.
Parent experiences of emotionally based school non-attendance. Develops framework for reintegration based on lived experience.
Preliminary RCT of CBT-based group play therapy for anxiety-related school refusal. Early evidence, limited sample and scope.
Online parenting program for adolescent school refusal. High acceptability but limited measurable improvement in attendance outcomes.
Qualitative study of multifamily therapy effects on parents. Emphasises relational and systemic change processes.
ROBINS-I tool for assessing bias in non-randomised intervention studies. Methodological standard rather than substantive EBSA content.
RoB 2 tool for assessing bias in randomised trials. Key methodological reference for intervention evaluation.
Links adverse childhood experiences to education outcomes. Indirect relevance to EBSA via risk factor pathways.
Multi-modal CBT-based treatment for chronic school refusal. Reports outcomes for structured clinical intervention.
Scoping review of school refusal and anxiety. Synthesises broad literature without strict intervention focus.
Grounded theory study of family coach interventions for persistent non-attendance. Focus on process rather than outcomes.
Systematic review of school refusal literature. Consolidates definitions, causes, and intervention approaches.
Examines school transition effects on wellbeing and attendance. Highlights developmental turning points in attendance trajectories.
